explanation: The property states that whenever a PCV-to-PSV switch is initiated (meaning PSVModeSelected transitions from false to true), the system shall immediately ask for confirmation/setting of PSV parameters. The first part of the formula, (G (((! PSVModeSelected) & (X PSVModeSelected)) -> (X confirmPSVParameters))), ensures that globally, if PSVModeSelected becomes true, then confirmPSVParameters will be true in the very next state. The second part, (PSVModeSelected -> confirmPSVParameters), covers the initial state, ensuring that if PSVModeSelected is true at the beginning, then confirmPSVParameters is also true at the beginning.

The property states that globally, whenever a PCV-to-PSV switch is initiated by the user (PSVModeSelected becomes true), it must be followed by the system asking for confirmation/setting of PSV parameters (confirmPSVParameters becoming true) in the same state. This ensures that the confirmation is immediately presented when the switch is initiated.

The LTL property states that globally, whenever a PCV-to-PSV switch is initiated by the user (PSVModeSelected), the system must concurrently ask the user for confirmation/setting of PSV parameters (confirmPSVParameters). This captures the immediate and mandatory requirement implied by 'When A, B shall happen'.

The property states that globally, whenever a PCV-to-PSV switch is initiated by the user (represented by `PSVModeSelected`), then in the very next state, the system must ask the user for confirmation/setting of PSV parameters (represented by `confirmPSVParameters`). This captures the immediate and mandatory response required after the initiation event.

The property captures that whenever the user initiates a switch to PSV mode (represented by 'PSVModeSelected' becoming true), the system must subsequently ask for confirmation or setting of PSV parameters ('confirmPSVParameters') within a specific time duration (N_DURATION). The LTL template handles both the case where the 'PSVModeSelected' event occurs as a transition (rising edge) and the case where it is already true at the initial state.

The property states that globally, whenever a PCV-to-PSV switch is initiated by the user, represented by the rising edge of `PSVModeSelected` (`(!PSVModeSelected & X PSVModeSelected)`), the system shall ask for confirmation/setting of PSV parameters (`confirmPSVParameters`). The `X (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3)` part ensures that the asking action occurs promptly within `N_DURATION` time units starting from the next state after the initiation event. The second part of the conjunction (`PSVModeSelected -> (F[0,N_DURATION] confirmPSVParameters)`) covers the initial state, ensuring that if the `PSVModeSelected` is true at time 0, the confirmation still occurs within the specified duration. `N_DURATION` is set to 1 to imply an immediate or very next-step response for "shall ask".

The property states that globally (G), whenever a PCV-to-PSV switch is initiated by the user (PSVModeSelected), the system must immediately ask the user for confirmation/setting of PSV parameters (confirmPSVParameters). This captures the 'When A, then B' temporal relationship.

The property states that globally (G), whenever a PCV-to-PSV switch is initiated by the user (PSVModeSelected), the system shall ask the user for confirmation/setting of PSV parameters to be used (confirmPSVParameters). This is captured by a global implication, where the initiation of the switch is the antecedent (bool_exp2) and the confirmation request is the consequent (bool_exp3).

The property ensures that globally, whenever a switch to PSV mode is initiated by the user (represented by 'PSVModeSelected' becoming true), the system will ask for confirmation or setting of PSV parameters in the very next state. Additionally, the second part ensures that if the system starts in a state where PSV mode is already selected, it must immediately be asking for confirmation/setting of PSV parameters.

The property states that globally, whenever a PCV-to-PSV switch is initiated by the user, the system must immediately ask the user for confirmation/setting of PSV parameters. This is captured by a universal guarantee (G) that an implication (->) holds: if the switch is initiated (bool_exp1), then the confirmation/setting action (bool_exp3) must occur.
